intro: The five tools a beginner is most likely to meet, in plain terms. There are hundreds of AI products; you can ignore almost all of them. Every card shows when it was last checked.

cards.name: ChatGPT
cards.plainDescription: The chatbot that started the boom, and still the most widely used. A strong all-rounder: writing, questions, brainstorming, images, and spoken conversation.
goodFor: Talking to it out loud (voice mode)
cards.freeAccess: Free to use; paid plan adds heavier use and newer features.

cards.name: Claude
cards.plainDescription: A chatbot known for careful, natural writing and strong reasoning. Particularly good with long documents and thoughtful, nuanced work. (Full disclosure: this site is maintained with Claude's help — see the trust section.)
goodFor: Writing and editing that sounds human
Coding help (Claude Code is a developer favorite)
cards.freeAccess: Free to use; paid plan adds heavier use and newer features.

cards.name: Gemini
cards.plainDescription: Google's chatbot, woven into the tools many people already use — Search, Gmail, Docs, and Android phones.
goodFor: Questions that benefit from fresh web results
Help inside Gmail and Google Docs
cards.freeAccess: Free to use; paid plan adds heavier use and newer features.

cards.name: Microsoft Copilot
cards.plainDescription: Microsoft's AI assistant, built into Windows, Edge, and (with a work or paid plan) Word, Excel, and Outlook. If your life runs on Office, this is the one you'll meet at work.
goodFor: Drafting and summarizing in Word and Outlook
cards.freeAccess: Free version available; the deeper work-data integration comes with paid or workplace plans.

cards.name: Perplexity
cards.plainDescription: An AI-powered answer engine: ask a question, get a direct answer with links to its sources. The most beginner-friendly way to research a topic without wading through search results.
goodFor: Research questions where you want sources shown
News and current events with citations
cards.freeAccess: Free to use; paid plan adds heavier use and newer features.
